What's in a name? Kerala

# What's in a name? Kerala

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 25, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

11

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

2

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

Kerala God’s own country, was called as Cheralam in classical Tamil, referring to Chera dynasty, who were also called as Kerala Putra. As per folklore, the state gets it’s name from Kera( coconut trees) and alam( land), meaning land of coconuts, owing to the abundant coconut trees here.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!fbNp!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F27157fcb-4dd1-4904-adc6-36c034d765b6_1280x735.jpeg)

![A collection of images of Kerala](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!fbNp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F27157fcb-4dd1-4904-adc6-36c034d765b6_1280x735.jpeg "A collection of images of Kerala")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Malabar was the name given by Arab merchants and travellers, like Al-Biruni. As per William Logan, Malabar combines Tamil Mala( Mountain) and Persian Arabic Barr( country, continent).

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2Osz!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd59804fe-8685-4734-9067-4aa1f598cf8e_1280x826.jpeg)

![Bekal Fort in Kasargod, Kerala](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2Osz!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd59804fe-8685-4734-9067-4aa1f598cf8e_1280x826.jpeg "Bekal Fort in Kasargod, Kerala")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_kKi!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe97c4666-d674-452f-b033-43204b510097_1280x917.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_kKi!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe97c4666-d674-452f-b033-43204b510097_1280x917.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Kasaragod, Kerala’s northern most district, bordering Karnataka, famous for Bekal Fort, is believed to have got it’s name from Kasara( a type of tree) and Gode( fort), meaning “Fort of Kasara trees”. As per another theory, the town was originally called Kāññirakkōṭŭ in Malayalam, meaning land of Kannira trees. There is another theory of being derived from a Sanskrit word meaning Lake( Kaasara) of Hidden Treasure(Kroda).

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!zjZ2!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4f523e72-fd42-448d-98a2-b84a2ea8078a_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!zjZ2!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4f523e72-fd42-448d-98a2-b84a2ea8078a_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vl5_!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7dc09075-1fb4-4d77-a0b6-7f3a4cfa5e9d_677x614.jpeg)

![Adi Kadalayi Shree Krishna Temple in Katalayi,Kannur - Best Temples near me in Kannur - Justdial](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vl5_!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7dc09075-1fb4-4d77-a0b6-7f3a4cfa5e9d_677x614.jpeg "Adi Kadalayi Shree Krishna Temple in Katalayi,Kannur - Best Temples near me  in Kannur - Justdial")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Kannur is believed to be named after Kannan Ur, meaning Place of Krishna, the place is known for it’s Adi Kadalayi Sree Krishna Temple at Katalayi.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Q9ix!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F780d9de7-f387-4a80-a46e-ce8161e04084_1280x853.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Q9ix!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F780d9de7-f387-4a80-a46e-ce8161e04084_1280x853.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cbGY!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa26ad0f8-8e0d-4b03-b8d2-21b1cd1a1b16_1280x848.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cbGY!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa26ad0f8-8e0d-4b03-b8d2-21b1cd1a1b16_1280x848.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Wayanad district, famous for it’s natural beauty, is derived from vayal nāḍŭ, which means Land of Paddy fields in Malayalam.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!CsGI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe3bc96cb-d774-43fa-87f7-2e9c4dbbc999_1280x850.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!CsGI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe3bc96cb-d774-43fa-87f7-2e9c4dbbc999_1280x850.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Sultan Bathery, Wayanad’s main commercial hub gets it’s name from the fact that Tipu Sultan used this as a place to store ( Battery) his ammunition during invasion of Malabar.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!kg9K!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffdb491cf-0421-4369-8a8f-784201a650e4_960x720.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!kg9K!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffdb491cf-0421-4369-8a8f-784201a650e4_960x720.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Kozhikode, the largest city in Malabar, known for it’s beach, IIM and NIT, is believed to have got it’s name from Koyil Kota meaning fortified palace. Calicut was what Arabs and English called it as. Calico a fine variety of hand-woven cotton exported from the port here, gets it’s name from Calicut.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!UIfy!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feb8ad14e-ae9a-43c2-82cd-06c8f54c6e3a_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!UIfy!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feb8ad14e-ae9a-43c2-82cd-06c8f54c6e3a_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!u6So!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb5fb9d62-2b4b-4a14-92b5-9ea24c3ae36a_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!u6So!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb5fb9d62-2b4b-4a14-92b5-9ea24c3ae36a_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yVNs!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F07294b00-3885-46ae-856b-000ca9764515_960x720.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yVNs!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F07294b00-3885-46ae-856b-000ca9764515_960x720.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Malappuram gets it’s name from a Malayalam word meaning Over the Hill, owing to the district’s rather hilly landscape consisting of ranges like Arimbra, Cheriyam, Pandalur.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TEOa!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fddd627da-bdca-4abd-8ca8-cbf14890fc84_1280x853.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TEOa!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fddd627da-bdca-4abd-8ca8-cbf14890fc84_1280x853.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Palakkad, was called as Palakkattussery, so called after Pala trees that once densely occupied this land.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oeSo!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7cf8175b-8da0-4d6c-8da1-702be7c05043_1280x862.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oeSo!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7cf8175b-8da0-4d6c-8da1-702be7c05043_1280x862.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xqnm!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F45b6da23-2614-491b-a2c6-7817b8c6cee0_750x447.jpeg)

![Vadakkumnathan Temple during the Maha Shivaratri festival](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xqnm!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F45b6da23-2614-491b-a2c6-7817b8c6cee0_750x447.jpeg "Vadakkumnathan Temple during the Maha Shivaratri festival")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Thrissur is the shortened form of 'Thrissivaperur”, town named after Shiva. It was also called as Vrishabhadripuram in ancient times. It is famous for the Vadakkunnathan Temple dedicated to Shiva.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!EAnI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F596c8071-6fa0-405f-acec-4f229f8a72b9_1141x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!EAnI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F596c8071-6fa0-405f-acec-4f229f8a72b9_1141x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Guruvayoor famous for the Krishna temple, is believed to have got it’s name from the fact that Guru( Brihaspati) and Vayu together installed the Krishna deity here, which they had got from Uddhava, who had retrived it after submergence of Dwarka.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!LHzm!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8fef6b9b-4ce4-47e0-9d03-bf3905efb905_1280x852.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!LHzm!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8fef6b9b-4ce4-47e0-9d03-bf3905efb905_1280x852.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Pathanamthitta district, where the famous Sabarimala temple is located gets it’s name from *pathanam* and *thitta,* meaning array of houses on the river side. The district is also famous for it’s boat races.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oLhl!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd017384a-0f8c-4923-93d3-1ca35a525c53_1024x449.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oLhl!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd017384a-0f8c-4923-93d3-1ca35a525c53_1024x449.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Ylv0!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe4f5cd12-7830-4fd0-b296-fc5303564333_960x700.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Ylv0!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe4f5cd12-7830-4fd0-b296-fc5303564333_960x700.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!26PC!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4f901d66-fc11-47bb-ae42-8ca84735c6a8_720x480.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!26PC!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4f901d66-fc11-47bb-ae42-8ca84735c6a8_720x480.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Ernakulam the district as well as twin city of Kochi is believed to have got it’s name from Erayanar another name of Shiva, while as per Komattil Achutha Menon, it’s believed to have got it’s name from Erangiyal, a type of mud.

Kochi is believed to have got it’s name from kochu azhi, meaning small lagoon in Malayalam. Though as per explorers like Nicolo Conti it gets it’s name from Kochi river connecting backwaters to the sea.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Dz7c!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F028fc98f-f97b-4bfb-a427-27337cc7a311_960x1152.jpeg)

![Top to bottom, left to right: MC Mathew memorial at Thirunakkara, Aksharashilpam, Immaculate Heart of Mary Cathedral, Kodimatha Boat Jetty, Kottayam Government Medical College, CMS College Kottayam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Dz7c!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F028fc98f-f97b-4bfb-a427-27337cc7a311_960x1152.jpeg "Top to bottom, left to right: MC Mathew memorial at Thirunakkara, Aksharashilpam, Immaculate Heart of Mary Cathedral, Kodimatha Boat Jetty, Kottayam Government Medical College, CMS College Kottayam")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Kottayam the center of Syrian Christianity, as well as being home to all leading Malayalam publishing and media homes, is believed to be derived from Kotta and Akam meaning “Interior of the fort”.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qn_e!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F711ac102-e508-46e6-89e2-3b8a6e2abb32_1246x769.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!qn_e!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F711ac102-e508-46e6-89e2-3b8a6e2abb32_1246x769.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!arDD!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0f9a46e0-32f8-4c9a-8e72-c172fb337191_1246x769.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!arDD!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0f9a46e0-32f8-4c9a-8e72-c172fb337191_1246x769.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Idduki famous for it’s natural beauty, hills and tea estates, gets it’s name from a Malayalam word meaning Gorge. Munnar, one of the main hill stations in South, gets it’s name from Munnu( Three) and Aaru( River), referring to confluence of 3 rivers-Muthirapuzha, Nallathanni and Kundali.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!dKsL!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F574632d8-0ca4-4e3b-9536-b87476560c58_960x716.jpeg)

![AC Road SH-11](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!dKsL!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F574632d8-0ca4-4e3b-9536-b87476560c58_960x716.jpeg "AC Road SH-11")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Alapphuza famous for it’s backwaters, canals gets it’s name from two Malayalam words Ala meaning “wide” and Puzha meaning “river”. Alleppey was the name in British time.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-UIk!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F45a6031f-ce44-49e0-bd63-cb8379ea1b44_450x300.jpeg)

![Red-and-white-striped lighthouse, behind a stand of palm trees](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!-UIk!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F45a6031f-ce44-49e0-bd63-cb8379ea1b44_450x300.jpeg "Red-and-white-striped lighthouse, behind a stand of palm trees")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Kollam famous for it’s beaches, lagoons, is believed to have got it’s name from a Malayalam word meaning “Black Pepper”, owing to the fact that the town was a hub of pepper trade. Another theory states that it was derived from kovilakam and Illam meaning palace house. It was also called Desinganadu, Venad, while during Portuguese times it was called Quilon.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!23xf!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa79530c0-de7d-4c0b-bf7e-d8cf53a204a1_1280x720.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!23xf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa79530c0-de7d-4c0b-bf7e-d8cf53a204a1_1280x720.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Aogo!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7e3263db-b1cc-452f-b041-58c6c4c27e43_1920x954.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Aogo!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7e3263db-b1cc-452f-b041-58c6c4c27e43_1920x954.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Thiruvananthapuram, the capital city famous for Ananta Padmanabhaswamy Temple, comes from a term meaning “Abode of Anantha”.

This is not an exhaustive list, more about some of the important cities and towns of Kerala.

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

11

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

2

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
